semantic.role: State persistence and lifecycle manager for the Spine manifest runtime state and language detection snapshots.
responsibilities: Persist and load runtime state (ManifestRuntimeState) to/from the manifest JSON file under `.spine/manifest.json`.
Save and load language detection snapshots (LanguageSnapshot) to/from the language snapshot file.
Determine if a manifest is in a 'virgin' state by checking the lastSync timestamp – used to decide whether to perform a full scan.
Check for the existence of a manifest baseline file to support incremental sync decisions.
Record and persist scan metadata (mode, duration, LLM provider/model) alongside runtime state and violation counts from a status source.
outOfScope: Manifest schema validation and integrity checks (handled by `infra/manifest/integrity.ts`).
Index document management or knowledge graph operations.
Triggering or orchestrating scan operations – this module only persists results.
invariants: All state files are persisted as JSON under the `.spine` directory using paths defined in `io.ts`.
The `saveManifestState` function requires a valid `ManifestStatusSource` with `getGlobalStatus` and `getActiveViolations` methods.
Language snapshots are written atomically via `FileSystemManager.safeWriteFile` to prevent partial writes.
The `reverseIndexComplete` flag is the only mutable runtime state field; all other fields are derived or metadata.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a centralized persistence layer for Spine manifest runtime state, decoupling state I/O from business logic and scan orchestration.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial production release of ArchSpine v1.0.0 — Architectural Governance & Semantic Layer for the AI Era; this module was created to support the core manifest state lifecycle.

publicSurface.description: Interface representing the persisted runtime state of the manifest, currently only containing a `reverseIndexComplete` flag.
Interface for an external status source that provides global status (`getGlobalStatus`) and active violation counts (`getActiveViolations`) for inclusion in saved manifest state.
Reads and returns the `ManifestRuntimeState` from the manifest JSON file, or returns a default state with `reverseIndexComplete: false` if the file does not exist.
Checks whether the manifest has ever been synced by examining the lastSyncAt timestamp; returns `true` if the manifest file is missing, empty, or has no valid sync timestamp.
Returns `true` if the manifest JSON file exists on disk and can be read, indicating a prior scan baseline is available.
Persists the `reverseIndexComplete` flag into the manifest file without overwriting other state, used to mark completion of reverse index derivation.
Writes the complete manifest runtime state, scan metadata (mode, duration, LLM provider/model), and current violation count from the status source to the manifest JSON file.
Loads and returns the `LanguageSnapshot` from the language snapshot file, or `null` if the file does not exist or cannot be parsed.
Saves the given `LanguageSnapshot` to the language snapshot file using atomic file write.

exports.name: saveManifestState
